problem.title: Merge Overlapping Intervals
problem.description: Given a collection of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals.

example.explanation: [1,3] and [2,4] overlap → [1,4]. Sort by start, merge if next.start ≤ current.end.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sorting [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Sort intervals by start time so overlapping intervals become adjacent, then perform a single linear pass merging each interval into the last result interval when they overlap. Two intervals overlap when the current end is greater than or equal to the next interval's start. Sorting is the key enabler — without it we would need O(n²) comparisons.
